heading: Authorization of appropriations; allocation of funds
label: 3336 Authorization of appropriations; allocation of funds
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

(a). substituted “subchapter—” for “subchapter, such sums not to exceed $10,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 1991 through 2012.” and added pars. (1) and (2).Pub. L. 113–792008—Subsec. L. 105–185, § 301(a)(16)Subsec. (b). , which directed that the second sentence of subsec. (b) be amended by striking out the last sentence, was executed by striking out “The Secretary shall, whenever possible, consult with the Board in developing plans for the use of these funds.”, which is both the second and last sentence of subsec. (b), to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 105–185, § 606(e)1996—Subsec. (a). substituted “1997” for “1995”.Pub. L. 104–1271990—Subsec. (a). substituted “for each of the fiscal years 1991 through 1995” for “annually for the period beginning , and ending ”.Pub. L. 101–624October 1, 1981September 30, 19901985—Subsec. (a). substituted “1990” for “1985, and thereafter such sums as may after the date of enactment of this subchapter be authorized by law for any subsequent fiscal year”.Pub. L. 99–198

children.text: $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1991 through 2013; and
$2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.

children.label: b
children.text: Funds appropriated under this section shall be allocated by the Secretary to eligible institutions for work to be done as mutually agreed upon between the Secretary and the eligible institution or institutions.
